title: Level 118

|body= '''''Loaded!''''' {{Alt Class|ent=0|env=3|ext=3|no=118}} {{Notice2|body=Changes: Our team managed to reach Level 118. We haven't found an exit and decided to log our stay in this god-forsaken level. And the photos were quite low quality so if we find any good examples we'll replace the old ones.}} '''Level 118 '''is the 119th level of the Backrooms.
{{Blurimage|file=118lonesakura.jpg|caption=I am so glad trees are blind.|hovertext=At least I know they aren't watching me too.}}
==Description:== '''Level 118''' is an ethereal garden, a grove of cherry blossom trees blanketed by pale fog, still now and forever. On most of '''Level 118''' the smooth mahogany trunks of dozens of cherry blossom trees can be witnessed in every direction, filling the air with a slightly sweet aroma. The only reprieve from their gaze is the near-infinite murky water present in '''Level 118'''. Their branches almost appear to glow in the fog, thin black branches forever outstretched towards the unseeable sky. You will never glimpse it, because of the silvery fog which chokes all of '''Level 118''' so thick its humid embrace brushes against your skin. This is not the fog’s only vice, as it smothers out any signal, making communication impossible for those lost inside. This smoky haze rises continually from the aforementioned lakes that cover a significant portion of '''Level 118'''.
Below your feet, the soft grass is green enough to look alive but lacks vibrancy. Nonetheless, it appears short and well-maintained, never growing as if some unseeable barrier is right above it. It and the trees will remain still, forever dreaming, as they always have. And can’t you hear the trees singing a haunting lullaby? Their fallen pale pink petals blanket the ground and dampen any noise that interrupts the silence.  Whilst petals can be found practically everywhere on '''Level 118''', patches of bare earth can lie hidden beneath them instead of the more elegant grass. Coarse, hideous, concrete can also be found, its failed attempts to imitate beauty are mostly unseen below the sea of cherry blossoms. Meanwhile, dappled petals float in place on the opaque surface of the frigid water whilst foaming gray waves silently crash against concrete walls that mark the water's edge. The sea churns silently for miles on end even after the fog hides it from sight. If you were to dive below the surface, you’d find the unlit shallows perfectly calm as you sink into the never-ending abyss below. 
{{Blurimage|file=118sakurapath.jpg|hovertext=Or another false hope?|caption=My... Salvation?}}

When traveling on '''Level 118''' several oddities can be noted that contribute to making the blossom so beautiful. First and foremost, not a single [[Object List|artifact]] nor useful item dares to intrude. Not even the tiniest scrap of trash, of humanity, shall ever be found within '''Level 118'''. Those trapped cannot change this as anything from outside you leave behind will be swallowed by the fog and vanish once you lose sight of it. Perhaps even when we die, even our corpses shall be taken, and the grove will seem unchanged. The last noticed, and perhaps the most unfortunate of all '''Level 118'''’s parts, is severe insomnia to a fatal degree, for the trees are sleeping alone, so why should we get the privilege to walk in dreams alongside them? After all, only the awake can marvel. But eventually, we will fall into a slumber and fall asleep forever. Perhaps even when we die, even our corpses shall be taken, and the grove will seem unchanged. The trees will sing gentle lullabies into eternity, as they always have. To it, humans are a change, a disgusting cancer that must be disposed of. '''Heaven''' does not welcome you, and it never will. ==Entities:== '''Level 118''' knows no entities, they are smart enough not to intrude. You were not, and now your mind will become the greatest hostility. There are no shadows in the fog, the water is calm, and the trees are not singing. But I can still hear their song. ==Colonies and Outposts:== Who would dare to make anything here? If they ever tried it would eventually rot, and then the fog would take it. Maybe they’ll send a team to look for you. Yet while you’re not [[Phenomenon 15|alone]], they will never find you. ==Entrances and Exits: == ===Entrances:=== Noclipping into a shorter tree on [[Level 70]] may lead here. One may find and pass through Torii gate on [[Level 182]] to arrive here. Noclipping through a flower patch on [[Level 427]] may lead here. ===Exits:=== Tiled staircases leading downwards will lead to [[Level 798]]. One can walk down a staircase in the ground to arrive in the exterior of [[Level 218]]. Arched tree patterns can sometimes lead one to [[Level 416]].
Log 1:
Date of Upload: 8 days ago

====Operation: Hedge Trimmers==== Mission Captain: Katherine Garcia Researcher: Anthony Bell Archivist: Matthew Box Mission Objective: Use thermal cameras in an attempt to check for possible entities caretaking the level. ''[[M.E.G.]] Operative Matthew Box here, I've been selected for a research mission in '''Level 118.''' Not that glamorous a position, I'm really just a research guy in a team of three. The sole reason I qualified is because I know my way with the database. I also managed to land the role of equipment carrier which I'm not too stoked about. '' ''From what I'm aware, it won't be too hard a task though, so I guess I have that to be thankful for. It's a simple research mission, two days total. Get in, set up thermal cameras, throw trash on the grass, see what happens, and leave. Boring? Yes. Useful? Remains to be seen. Should be simple enough, eh? I am kind of concerned about how sleep deprivation will affect the journey. I'm not at all familiar with insomnia. Shoot- Commander Lucia says it's time to move out.''
Log 2:
Date of Upload: 7 days ago

''Matthew again here... We're a day in and already something went wrong because of course, it would. We managed to set up the cameras without a hitch thankfully and went through 3 trials before it all went to hell. The first two turned up nothing. The trash we placed was gone but the cameras caught nothing. Commander insisted we keep trying because maybe it was too fast or something. Personally, I'd think if there was anything it wouldn't have body heat in the first place. The only useful thing we could be doing with this place is turning it into a trash disposal system. '' ''I guess we were slightly too late on the third because it took the cameras. Anthony was pretty pissed because those things weren't easy to make. I'm just glad it's not coming out of my paycheck. I stopped to look around and because my life can never go perfectly, I got separated from the rest of the team. Not the worst level to be stuck in, but I'd rather not be stuck alone on any level. I fired up the emergency radio. (Seems like carrying all the supplies does have its perks). No idea when the retrieval team will arrive. Hey, maybe they'll even send two if Katherine contacts them. Everything's going to be alright, I know it.''
Log 3:
Date of Upload: 5 days ago

''It's Matthew again, and I'm still trapped which is simply wonderful. I have no idea how much time has passed, and I have no idea how to feel about that. I'd guess I haven't slept in at least two days, but my opinion seems to be of no value whatsoever. I'm starting to get tired, even though I've stayed in one place. (Believe it or not, I can think intelligent thoughts every once and a while). Thankfully I have food and [[Object 1|Almond Water]] on me so I won't be starving or dying of thirst. Hardly a blessing right now but I'll take it. It'll keep me going long enough to find the exit, which is what matters. '' ''I really hope I get out soon. I hope my teammates are alright... they probably are but knowing what's happening to me I can't be sure. Go looking for monsters and they just might find you. I know this place has no entities... or at least ones that want to be seen. (And I'll respect introverts). I keep seeing shadows rushing through the fog. And my eyes see the leaves shake and crawl along the grass. It has to be all in my head. It can't be real. On a... more depressing note, I found out that there's no Wi-Fi here. The SOS signal and probably even these logs are not reaching anyone. Nobody's coming to save me. I have to do things myself which maybe this time will somehow end well. I can't die here...''
Log 4:
Date of Upload: 2 days ago

''I don't know how much time is left before I end up dead. I heard you can die after at least three days of not sleeping. Regardless of how annoying I may be, I doubt that's desirable. '''Level 118''' unsettles me with how perfect and utterly empty it is, and it feels like it makes sense in a way I don't understand. I'm exhausted and it's putting me on edge. But I'm considering that exhaustion is the least of my worries right now. Those damned shadows in the fog are more frequent. They're watching me... laughing at me. '' ''My skin doesn't hurt but when I look down something is crawling under it. I froze up when I saw it. If I wasn't conserving food, I'd probably have hurled. Why can't those things just kill me yet? They have to be enjoying my suffering, I know it. At least I can be thankful that the fog means I won't see all of them, even if that brings its own type of creepiness. I need to get out of here... but I'm starting to wonder if all the exits have closed up. It would be just my luck after all. All I can tell everything is hostile. I'd rather not think my mind could invent some of the things I've seen. God... I can't think straight. I'm scared...''
Log 5:
Date of Upload: 6 minutes ago

''Do you ever think about how every day the sun crawls along the sky to shine its light down on us? The sky is so big, yet it journeys every day to fill us with warmth. I think I need that sunlight right now. They're everywhere. Those damn things are everywhere, watching me. I need to get out now. Why can't that light cleanse this place forsaken by God? But I have to wonder if it would think of the impurities as those things or me. Am I a stain on this perfectly imperfect... man-less world? Humans were never meant to be here. Here is enlightenment. To sleep is to close your eyes. I wish I could close them again and sleep through this hell. But somehow, I found my safety. A way out. I think, if my thoughts have any value, that this one will lead to [[Level 798|that garden]]. I'm finally free. ''
}}
